label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's start with reading the problem carefully. We have a table of penguins with the following columns: name, age, height (cm), weight (kg). The initial penguins in the table are: - Louis, 7, 50, 11 - Bernard, 5, 80, 13 - Vincent, 9, 60, 11 - Gwen, 8, 70, 15 So, the initial data is: | Name | Age | Height (cm) | Weight (kg) | |--------|-----|-------------|-------------| | Louis | 7 | 50 | 11 | | Bernard| 5 | 80 | 13 | | Vincent| 9 | 60 | 11 | | Gwen | 8 | 70 | 15 | Then, we add a new penguin named James, who is 12 years old, 90 cm tall, and weighs 12 kg: | Name | Age | Height (cm) | Weight (kg) | |--------|-----|-------------|-------------| | Louis | 7 | 50 | 11 | | Bernard| 5 | 80 | 13 | | Vincent| 9 | 60 | 11 | | Gwen | 8 | 70 | 15 | | James | 12 | 90 | 12 | Then, we delete the penguin named Bernard from the table: | Name | Age | Height (cm) | Weight (kg) | |--------|-----|-------------|-------------| | Louis | 7 | 50 | 11 | | Vincent| 9 | 60 | 11 | | Gwen | 8 | 70 | 15 | | James | 12 | 90 | 12 | Now, we are to find out which penguin is the oldest. Let's look at the ages of the remaining penguins: - Louis: 7 years old - Vincent: 9 years old - Gwen: 8 years old - James:
